key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share
key_assumptions.source: FY2025 operating cash flow $4812.2M less capital spending $533.6M ÷ 496.709M diluted shares per ADI/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20251125_rpt20251101_acc0000006281_25_000153.htm
Mid-cycle analog recovery with industrial and automotive mix

open_questions: Confirm AI/data-center milestone band (**$0–$45/sh**) versus industrial revenue already in consolidated free cash flow.
Cycle and integration reserve band (**−$35 to −$10/sh**) pending next inventory correction.
Live price and final base IRR after mechanical refresh.
thesis: Analog Devices, Inc. designs, manufactures, and markets high-performance analog, mixed-signal, and digital signal processing integrated circuits, software, and subsystems. **[Fact]** Shares trade on Nasdaq (ADI). **[Fact]** FY2025 consolidated revenue was **$11.0 billion**, operating income **$2.93 billion**, and net income **$2.27 billion** (diluted earnings **$4.56** per share) per `ADI/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20251125_rpt20251101_acc0000006281_25_000153.htm`. **[Fact]** FY2025 cash from operations was **$4.81 billion**, capital spending **$0.53 billion**, and free cash flow about **$4.28 billion** on roughly **496.7 million** diluted shares (**$8.61** per share).
why_market_wrong: The stock may trade as a post-Maxim analog oligopolist while the market underweights cyclical recovery and overweights peak-cycle memories from FY2023. **[Inference]** FY2025 revenue rose **17%** from the FY2024 trough (**$9.43 billion** to **$11.02 billion**), yet generalists may still anchor on inventory-correction headlines rather than **$8.61** per share of filing-locked free cash flow. **[Inference]** Proof-first component math at base case sums to about **$210** per share of economic value versus a price near **$372**, suggesting the market prices analog moat durability, AI/data-center power content, and capital returns beyond what the conservative component schedule captures today. **[Inference]** Net debt of roughly **$6.1 billion** after the Maxim acquisition is material but serviceable against **$4.28 billion** of annual free cash flow.
